codeReview.summary: Feature intent is mostly implemented (explicit mission-not-found path, packet-ID upsert, approval/terminal guards, and mission-cancel seam), but dispatch gating is enforced too late: review-run launch can occur before approval/terminal rejection, so blocked dispatches can still create daemon-managed runs.

issues.description: missionDispatchPacket launches reviewRunLauncher before store-level approval/terminal checks run. In default server wiring, reviewRunLauncher is always set (OpenBurnBarDaemonServer.swift:57-66), while the new guards are inside store.dispatchMissionPacket (MissionControlStore.swift:558-564). Result: unapproved/cancelled/completed/failed missions can still spawn daemon-managed runs before missionNotApproved/missionTerminal is thrown, violating the requirement that dispatch be blocked for unapproved/terminal missions.

New VAL-DAEMON-009 tests validate error returns for unapproved/terminal missions but do not exercise the code path with a non-nil reviewRunLauncher, so they miss the side-effect regression where blocked dispatch still launches a run.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Skill procedure step requiring commit-id verification appears unenforced or was skipped: handoff commitId is not a valid git object, which broke direct `git show <commitId>` review flow.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: mission-core-worker SKILL.md step 6 requires `git rev-parse --verify <commitId>` and `git show --name-only <commitId>`, but transcript skeleton has no such commands and EndFeatureRun reports commitId `06c1a2a3e0b89c74f5d6e1a3b7c9f2d8e5a4b6c1` (invalid in repo; actual commit is `06c1a2a30124ed4468e0ab33904a38ffbe0d3259`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Architecture state-machine guidance and implemented behavior diverge for approval/cancellation semantics, which can mislead future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md states `mission.approve` outside `awaiting_approval` should reject (line 51) and `mission.cancel` is legal only from non-terminal states (line 44), while this feature's tests explicitly approve a cancelled mission and expect success (OpenBurnBarMissionControlServiceTests.swift:1016-1024), and missionCancel has no terminal-state guard (MissionControlStore.swift:513-525).

summary: Reviewed feature m6-planner-upgrade-and-dispatch-gates and found a blocking correctness bug: dispatch rejections are evaluated after run launch, allowing side effects for unapproved/terminal missions. Also noted a non-blocking test coverage gap for launcher-enabled dispatch and two shared-state gaps (invalid handoff commitId workflow and architecture/implementation mismatch on mission lifecycle semantics).
